title: CEO Commentary on Loan Condition – As of December 31, 2026
content:
CAMPUS's loan portfolio remains actively managed and appropriately reserved, increased modestly during the period. Total loans of $6,657,940 remain concentrated in First Lien Real Estate and New Vehicle lending segments.

Portfolio Composition

CAMPUS's loan portfolio continues to be primarily concentrated in real estate and vehicle lending categories. As of December 31, 2026, First Lien Real Estate totaled $1,878,220 (28.2%) and New Vehicle totaled $1,577,110 (23.7%), together representing approximately 51.9% of the total portfolio.

All other segments combined account for the remaining balance. Overall loan balances increased 0.2% from the previous period.

Delinquency & Credit Quality

Total delinquent loans decreased to approximately $6,752, a minimal amount relative to the overall portfolio size. The largest delinquency exposure remains in First Lien Real Estate ($184) and New Vehicle ($0).

The largest delinquency exposures are concentrated in real estate lending segments that also represent the dominant share of total loan balances. Management continues to monitor delinquency migration trends and collection performance to limit future credit deterioration.
